findings.detail: Updated Windows/WSL runtime wording to reflect Codex App main -> SSH remote -> devmgmt-wsl -> Linux-native Codex CLI.
Updated local environment guidance to prefer wsl.localhost and treat \wsl$ as legacy evidence.
Introduced only thin helper modules for authority/status/report/path/subprocess/redaction duplication. Public CLI entrypoints remain stable.
Added explicit app-usability orchestration and purpose-specific readiness semantics without weakening code-modification startup gates.
Governed docs and generated AGENTS were updated to use primary_user_control_surface and remote_session_control_surface wording.

decisions: workspace_authority.json remains the root manifest and module registry.
Serena and Context7 policies stay as existing startup-gate contracts instead of adding a duplicate startup_gates_policy.json.
toolchain_policy.json is extended instead of creating a shadow toolchain_surface_policy.json.
repair boundaries and score policy remain sections of workspace_authority.json, with user_score_policy.json and disqualifier_policy.json still referenced as canonical inputs.
activate_codex_app_usability.py orchestrates existing checks and repairs; it does not become an independent authority.
remaining_external_warnings: Serena onboarding and activation may still warn or block general code modification while app-usability remains ready-with-warnings.
Windows/WSL Git drift and client-side PATH contamination remain non-authority warnings.
